examples.title: Virtual Attribute Inheritance Example
examples.description: Demonstrates how virtual attribute is inherited through derived classes automatically
examples.explanation: Shows that once virtual is declared in base, all overriding functions remain virtual. The override keyword is optional but recommended for clarity and compile-time error checking.

examples.title: Pure Virtual Function and Abstract Class
examples.description: Demonstrates abstract class with pure virtual function
examples.explanation: Shape is abstract because it has a pure virtual function (= 0). Cannot instantiate Shape, but can create pointers to Shape and point to derived class objects.

examples.title: Virtual Destructor Demonstration
examples.description: Shows importance of virtual destructor in polymorphic base classes
examples.explanation: With virtual destructor, both Derived and Base destructors are called in correct order. Without it, only Base destructor executes causing memory leak in Derived class.

examples.title: Slicing Problem and Prevention
examples.description: Demonstrates object slicing and how to prevent it using pointers/references
examples.explanation: Slicing occurs when assigning derived object to base by value. Use pointers or references to base class to maintain polymorphic behavior.

examples.title: Override and Final Keywords
examples.description: Demonstrates C++11 override and final keywords for safer virtual functions
examples.explanation: override keyword ensures compile-time checking of override correctness. final keyword prevents further overriding of virtual functions.
